characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Little Bear is an imaginative, curious, active young cub living in the Canadian woods with his mother and father. He enjoys exploring the outdoors, inventing new games, and makes many unusual friends. Many of his adventures are left up to the viewer to decide if they are real or not, however. Beary Friendly: He is very benevolent and even when he's mad, he doesn't tend to say anything rude. Beary Funny: He's the subject of many jokes, a lot of them stemming from his childish naivety or his fondness for humour. Big Eater: There's little he doesn't like and he's always up for a snack, even eating the meal of stew, cookies, and jam his parents packed for his grandparents (with the help of his friends, though). Birthday Episode: In "Birthday Soup", he has his birthday and, finding no cake, decides to make birthdaysoupinstead. He gets cake as well, though.

characters.name: Little Bear
characters.description: Little Bear is an imaginative, curious, active young cub living in the Canadian woods with his mother and father. He enjoys exploring the outdoors, inventing new games, and makes many unusual friends. Many of his adventures are left up to the viewer to decide if they are real or not, however. Beary Friendly: He is very benevolent and even when he's mad, he doesn't tend to say anything rude. Beary Funny: He's the subject of many jokes, a lot of them stemming from his childish naivety or his fondness for humour. Big Eater: There's little he doesn't like and he's always up for a snack, even eating the meal of stew, cookies, and jam his parents packed for his grandparents (with the help of his friends, though). Birthday Episode: In "Birthday Soup", he has his birthday and, finding no cake, decides to make birthdaysoupinstead. He gets cake as well, though.

characters.name: Mother Bear
characters.description: Mother Bear is a warm-hearted homemaker who spends her days running the household, often while Father Bear is away at work, and often going along with Little Bear's silliness and adventures. Beary Friendly: She's just as affectionate and polite as her son. Feminine Women Can Cook: She does all the cooking and is the most stereotypically feminine of the cast. Good Parents: With Father Bear. She is a very patient and affectionate parent. Happily Married: With Father Bear, of course. They get along very well and are often seen displaying affection to each other.

characters.name: Father Bear
characters.description: Little Bear's father, who works as a fisherman. Because of his job, he is often away at sea; however, he is home for a lot of episodes, wherein he joins in with Little Bear's games, and occasionally he also brings Little Bear out for adventures on his boat with him. Beary Friendly: He's just as amicable as his wife and son. Butt-Monkey: He occasionally gets this trope. The highlight episodes are; “Feathers in a Bunch”, “Monster Pudding”, “Family Bath Time”, & “Snowball Fight”. Good Parents: With Mother Bear. He's just as patient and affectionate with his son as she is. Happily Married: With Mother Bear, of course. He is a very loving husband to her.

characters.name: Grandmother and Grandfather Bear
characters.description: Little Bear's grandparents, on Mother Bear's side. Beary Friendly: They're just as friendly as the rest of the Bear family. Cool Old Guy: Little Bear likes to play with Grandpa Bear. Cool Old Lady: Little Bear really likes spending time with Grandmother Bear, especially because of her stories. Good Parents: They maintain a good relationship with Mother Bear, and several episodes focus on one or both of them looking after Little Bear.

characters.name: Rusty Bear
characters.description: Little Bear's uncle, and Father Bear's younger brother. He does not live in the local area and does not have a family of his own or even a house; he sleeps in the woods. He is friendly with his nephew, brother, and sister in law, and visits occasionally, such as to help build Emily and Granny's cabin. Beary Friendly: Like the rest of his family, he is friendly and kind. Big Little Brother: Rusty is the biggest and tallest of any bear on the show, and Little Bear invokes this trope when Father Bear introduces the head-taller Rusty as his little brother, saying, "You’re the biggest little brother I’ve ever seen!" Cool Uncle: Little Bear seems fascinated by his rustic lifestyle and tries to emulate him whenever he visits. Unknown Relative: Little Bear meets his uncle for the first time in 'Building A House For Emily', and he appears not to have even known he had an uncle before that.

characters.name: Duck
characters.description: Duck is clumsy, slow-witted yet smart and well-meaning and always up for Little Bear's games. Big Eater: There's little she doesn't like, and the first thing she says to Little Bear when she arrives at his party is "Happy Birthday, Little Bear!Where's the cake?" Book Dumb: She doesn't seem to be very good with counting past three, and "E" is the only part of Emily's name she knows to spell with, however, she doesn't seem outrightstupid. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: She's very spacy, comes up with some odd ideas, and thinks painting Hen's house with pink polka dots is a nice idea. A Dog Named "Dog": A duck named "Duck".

characters.name: Hen
characters.description: Hen is a prim and logical friend to the more imaginative, silly Little Bear. A Dog Named "Dog": A hen named "Hen". Foil: She is more grounded and grown-up than the more free-spirited, young Little Bear. Hollywood Tone-Deaf: "Diva Hen" shows that she cannot sing at all. She's well aware of this fact. When she gains confidence to do her opera show, everyone covers their ears during the performance. Proper Lady: She's tidy and has very good manners.

characters.name: Cat
characters.description: Cat is very sarcastic, enjoys napping, and causing mischief. Beware the Nice Ones: Cat is easily the calmest and most laid-back, but when he gets sick over the others messing around when it's time to get their picture taken he snaps a little. Brutal Honesty: Cat is never unkind but he's also unafraid to tell you exactly what he thinks. A running gag in "Diva Hen":Hen, crying:You didn't like it!Little Bear:Oh, no! It's really...interesting!Hen:Youdidlike it!Cat:No.Hen:I knew it!Little Bear:Cat!Cat:What?Hen:Birdssing. Why can't chickens sing?!Cat:Because they can't.Little Bear:Cat!Cat:What? Cats Are Snarkers: As well as being the snarkiest cast member, he's the only cat.. Deadpan Snarker: Cat is the most sarcastic of the bunch.Hen:Little Bear's got the hiccup!Cat:Veryobservant, Hen.

characters.name: Owl
characters.description: Owl is rather pompous, but wise. He lives in a tree-house and enjoys reading. Comically Missing the Point: He does this a lot in spite of his intellect.Little Bear: (covered in white paint) Who AM I?Owl: You're a polar bear.Little Bear: A polar bear? Don't you know me, Owl?Owl: I-I don't know any polar bears... Furry Reminder: Mainly speaks, but can sometimes hoot, such as in "Duck Loses Her Quack". Gender Flip: In the Polish dub, Owl is female as Polish word for "owl" is feminine. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He is a little pompous and grumpy, but he still means well.

characters.name: Emily
characters.description: Emily is a seven-year-old human girl staying with her granny not far from Little Bear's house. Being the oldest, she likes being the boss and playing by the rules. Companion Cube: Her china doll, Lucy. Cool Big Sis: To Little Bear and the others. She enjoys doting on them, supports Little Bear's ideas for games and adventures, and gives him advice. Demoted to Extra: She only makes sporadic appearances in seasons 4 and 5. Leitmotif: Whenever Emily "talks" to Lucy, the latter's "dialogue" is represented by the flute.

characters.name: Granny
characters.description: Emily's grandmother who doesn't live too far from the Bears. She's had Little Bear over often and enjoys indulging in his and her granddaughter's adventures. Cool Old Lady: She's a free-spirited, laid-back grandma who flies a plane. Supreme Chef: She makes a mean batch of applesauce. Token Human: The only human on the show besides Emily.

characters.name: Mitzi
characters.description: Mitzi is a mischievous and tomboyish monkey who likes to explore. Informed Species: Mitzi lacks a tail, making her resemble a gibbon more than a monkey. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: She can be slightly more rude, brash and troublemaking than Little Bear's other friends, but at heart she's a nice and well-meaning monkey. Misplaced Wildlife: She's a monkey living in the woodlands of North America, with no indication of how she got there. Odd Name Out: She has a proper name instead of just beingnamed for her specieslike most of Little Bear's other animal friends.

characters.name: The Mermaid
characters.description: No Name Given: Her name is never revealed. Our Mermaids Are Different: She averts most traits of mermaid lore, such as being a siren. True Blue Femininity: Her hair, tail, and shirt are various shades of teal.

characters.name: No-Feet
characters.description: A little green snake Little Bear met once. Meaningful Name: He named himself "No-Feet" because he doesn't have feet, being a snake. Vegetarian Carnivore: Despite that snakes are carnivores, No-Feet is a vegetarian as he eats vegetables in the garden, but his favorite food is peas.

characters.name: The Ducklings
characters.description: Cheerful Child: They like to run around happily. A Lizard Named "Liz": One of them is named Little Peep after the noise he makes.

characters.name: Frog
characters.description: Catchphrase: Frequently says, "Om". A Dog Named "Dog": Is a frog whose actual name is Frog. Furry Reminder: He both speaks and croaks.
